However, current literature generally recognizes that the **peripheral zone**, not the central zone, is the most common site of prostate cancer initiation. Option D: "It is not a hormone dependent cancer"

- [UMLS_R7_L3_C2262]: States testosterone propionate is contraindicated in prostate hyperplasia due to androgenic effects → implies hormonal dependence.
- Androgens influence growth of prostate cells → strongly indicative of hormone dependency.
Thus, prostate cancer is well-known to be hormonally driven (androgen-dependent).
→ So saying it is NOT hormone-dependent is false.
